security_gap.gap_description: Vote input may be spoofed without signature verification.

actor_risk.description: The actor is unauthenticated and can perform governance actions.

behavioral_risk.rationale: The system accepts unsigned votes from external actors.

assumptions: The governance contract does not enforce signature verification for vote messages.
The actor is able to access the voting interface without authentication.
The governance contract does not implement rate limiting or other anti-spam measures for vote submissions

mitigation.strategy: Require signature-based vote authentication and role validation.

description: An attacker may impersonate a user and submit fraudulent votes to the governance contract.

security_gap.gap_description: Votes may be manipulated without proper validation.

actor_risk.description: The actor is unauthenticated and can perform governance actions.

behavioral_risk.rationale: The system does not sufficiently validate votes against system state.

assumptions: The governance contract does not implement integrity checks on vote data.
The actor is able to submit malformed or tampered vote messages.
There's a lack of input sanitization to prevent malicious data injection in vote messages

mitigation.strategy: Implement data validation checks on vote parameters and state before processing.

description: An attacker might be able to manipulate vote data and affect the outcome of proposals.

security_gap.gap_description: The system might be vulnerable to flooding attacks impacting proposal processing.

actor_risk.description: The actor is unauthenticated and can perform governance actions.

behavioral_risk.rationale: The system's vote processing may not be resilient against high-volume requests.

assumptions: The governance contract does not implement rate limiting to protect against flood attacks.
The actor can submit a large number of spurious vote requests.
No measures are in place to filter or throttle unusually high volume of vote requests

mitigation.strategy: Implement rate limiting and request throttling measures to prevent denial of service attacks.

description: An attacker might be able to overload the system by submitting a huge number of votes, thus hindering the governance process.
